items.title: Rana turns heads as Tigers ready for the kill
items.summary: Rana is a sensation unlike any before in Bangladesh cricket.

items.title: Mushfiqur, Taijul nominated for ICC Player of the Month
items.summary: Bangladesh's Mushfiqur Rahim and Taijul Islam, along with Nepal all-rounder Dipendra Singh Airee, have been nominated for the ICC Men's Player of the Month award for May 2026 following a string of impressive performances.

items.title: Cricketer Nasir, wife Tamima acquitted in adultery case
items.summary: Ex-husband filed the case, alleging that Tamima married Nasir without divorcing him

items.title: Australia seek response as Bangladesh close in on landmark series win
items.summary: Confident hosts will hope for another complete performance as they eye a maiden ODI series win against Australia

items.title: Joty remains upbeat despite warm-up defeats
items.summary: The Bangladesh skipper highlighted the performances of young opener Juairiya Ferdous and spinner Sanjida Akter Meghla, expressing optimism that the team will be better prepared when the tournament gets underway.

items.title: ‘We just weren’t able to keep him out of the game’: Carey on Rana
items.summary: He channelled that fire into a series of seriously fast spells on Tuesday, registering the best bowling figures by a Bangladesh bowler against Australia in ODIs, finishing with four for 41.

items.title: Ireland beat Bangladesh; Scotland thump Pakistan; NZ ace 184 chase vs SA
items.summary: Canning and Maguire starred for Ireland, while Bryce's 43-ball 94 took Scotland to a DLS win

items.title: 'My best match at this level' - Mosaddek revels in successful Bangladesh comeback
items.summary: In his first international appearance since November 2022, he scored 86* and picked up two wickets as Bangladesh beat Australia for only the second time in ODIs

items.title: Rana and Mosaddek star as Bangladesh end 21-year wait with crushing win
items.summary: Australia dropped four catches then lost early wickets in the chase before Nahid Rana's pace did the damage

items.title: ‘Playing county cricket is a dream come true’
items.summary: Hasan Mahmud has become the first cricketer from Bangladesh to play county cricket for Kent, the English county said on Tuesday following his signing.  The right-arm pacer will play in six Rothesay County Championship matches, starting in June and running until the end of the season.

items.title: ‘Huge opportunity’ to end Australia ODI hoodoo
items.summary: The Tigers remain winless against Australia across six home ODIs.

items.title: Australia bowl first in series opener, hand Liam Scott debut
items.summary: Mosaddek Hossain returns to the side after a gap of nearly four years as Bangladesh look to extend a strong home record

items.title: Mehidy confident of taking on Australia on green-tinged pitch
items.summary: Both Mehidy and Inglis welcomed the seam-friendly nature of the Mirpur pitch on the eve of the series opener

items.title: Confident Bangladesh pose Australia a fresh challenge after 15-year wait for an ODI series
items.summary: While spin dominated in Pakistan, Australia could find pace playing more of a role this series

items.title: Head out of Bangladesh tour, Marsh to miss ODIs; Murphy called up
items.summary: Head rested from the entire Bangladesh tour while Australia hope Marsh can recover from his ankle injury in time for the T20 series

items.title: Tamim: Building a world-class High Performance Centre 'my biggest dream'
items.summary: The newly elected BCB president also hopes to restore the reputation of Bangladesh cricket

items.title: Tamim Iqbal elected new BCB president
items.summary: The former Bangladesh captain will serve a four-year term, having already completed two months as the president of an ad-hoc committee

items.title: Women's T20 World Cup 2026 warm-up: Kapp, Wolvaardt take SA past Ireland
items.summary: Scotland thumped Netherlands by 58 runs, Athapaththu helped SL beat Pakistan, and NZ eased past Bangladesh

items.title: Nepal, Malaysia, Hong Kong, Oman qualify for Asian Games men's cricket competition
items.summary: The four teams will join Afghanistan, Bangladesh, India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka and Japan in the ten-team competition starting September 24
